Module lifeline
Package model.graph

Class Graph

java.lang.Object
model.graph.Graph
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Graph extends Object implements Serializable
Represents a graph using an adjacency list structure. The graph stores nodes and the edges connecting them.
See Also:
  • Serialized Form

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Graph()
    Creates an empty graph.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addEdge(Edge edge)
    Adds an edge to the graph.
    void
    addNode(Node node)
    Adds a node to the graph if it is not already present.
    void
    clear()
    Removes all nodes and edges from the graph.
    List<Edge>
    getAllEdges()
    Returns all edges in the graph.
    Set<Node>
    getAllNodes()
    Returns all nodes currently contained in the graph.
    List<Edge>
    getEdges(Node node)
    Returns all outgoing edges of a node.
    List<Node>
    getNeighbors(Node node)
    Returns all neighboring nodes reachable from a given node.
    Node
    getNodeById(int id)
    Finds a node from its identifier.
    boolean
    hasConnection(Node a, Node b)
    Checks whether two nodes are already connected in either direction.
    boolean
    hasEdge(Node source, Node destination)
    Checks whether a directed edge already exists from source to destination.
    void
    removeEdge(Node source, Node destination)
    Removes an edge between the source node and the destination node.
    void
    removeNode(Node node)
    Removes a node from the graph and deletes all edges connected to it.
    void
    replaceWith(Graph other)
    Replaces the current graph data with another graph.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Graph

      public Graph()
      Creates an empty graph.

  • Method Details

    • addNode

      public void addNode(Node node)
      Adds a node to the graph if it is not already present.
      Parameters:
      node - node to add

    • removeNode

      public void removeNode(Node node)
      Removes a node from the graph and deletes all edges connected to it.
      Parameters:
      node - node to remove

    • getAllNodes

      public Set<Node> getAllNodes()
      Returns all nodes currently contained in the graph.
      Returns:
      set of graph nodes

    • addEdge

      public void addEdge(Edge edge)
      Adds an edge to the graph. If the edge is not oriented, the reverse edge is also stored so the graph can be traversed in both directions.
      Parameters:
      edge - edge to add
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if one of the nodes does not belong to the graph

    • getEdges

      public List<Edge> getEdges(Node node)
      Returns all outgoing edges of a node.
      Parameters:
      node - source node
      Returns:
      list of outgoing edges

    • getNeighbors

      public List<Node> getNeighbors(Node node)
      Returns all neighboring nodes reachable from a given node.
      Parameters:
      node - source node
      Returns:
      list of neighboring nodes

    • getNodeById

      public Node getNodeById(int id)
      Finds a node from its identifier.
      Parameters:
      id - identifier of the node
      Returns:
      matching node, or null if no node has this identifier

    • hasEdge

      public boolean hasEdge(Node source, Node destination)
      Checks whether a directed edge already exists from source to destination.
      Parameters:
      source - source node
      destination - destination node
      Returns:
      true if an edge already exists from source to destination

    • hasConnection

      public boolean hasConnection(Node a, Node b)
      Checks whether two nodes are already connected in either direction.
      Parameters:
      a - first node
      b - second node
      Returns:
      true if a connection already exists between the two nodes

    • removeEdge

      public void removeEdge(Node source, Node destination)
      Removes an edge between the source node and the destination node.
      Parameters:
      source - starting node
      destination - ending node

    • getAllEdges

      public List<Edge> getAllEdges()
      Returns all edges in the graph.
      Returns:
      list of all edges

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Removes all nodes and edges from the graph. Since edges are stored inside the adjacency list, clearing the list removes the whole graph structure.

    • replaceWith

      public void replaceWith(Graph other)
      Replaces the current graph data with another graph.
      Parameters:
      other - graph to copy
